This prophecy was fulfilled in Christ. Let our souls rely on him and rejoice in him; then, for his sake, the Father will be pleased with us. The Holy Spirit not only came but rested upon him without measure. He patiently bore the contradiction of sinners. His kingdom is spiritual; he was not to appear with earthly honors. He is gentle toward those oppressed with doubts and fears, as a bruised reed; those who are as smoking flax, as the wick of a lamp newly lighted, which is ready to go out again. He will not despise them nor lay upon them more work or more suffering than they can bear. By a long course of miracles and his resurrection, he fully showed the truth of his holy religion. By the power of his gospel and grace, he fixes principles in the minds of men that tend to make them wise and just. The most distant nations wait for his law, wait for his gospel, and will welcome it. If we want to make our calling and election sure and have the Father delight over us for good, we must behold, hear, believe in, and obey Christ.

The work of redemption brings man back to the obedience he owes to God as his Maker. Christ is the light of the world. And by his grace, he opens understandings that Satan has blinded and sets people free from the bondage of sin. The Lord has supported his church. Now he makes new promises that will be fulfilled just as certainly as the old ones were. When the Gentiles are brought into the church, he is glorified in them and by them. Let us give to God those things which are his, taking heed that we do not serve creation more than the Creator.

The Lord will appear in his power and glory. He will cry out in the preaching of his word. He will cry aloud in gospel warnings, which must be preached with gospel blessings, to awaken a sleeping world. He will conquer by the power of his Spirit. And those who contradict and blaspheme his gospel, he will put to silence and shame; and that which hinders its progress will be taken out of the way. To those who were naturally blind, God will show the way to life and happiness through Jesus Christ. They are weak in knowledge, but He will make darkness light. They are weak in duty, but their way will be plain. Those whom God brings into the right way, he will guide in it. This passage is a prophecy, and it also applies to every believer; for the Lord will never leave nor forsake them.

Observe the call given to this people and the character given of them. Multitudes are ruined for want of observing that which they cannot but see; they perish not through ignorance, but carelessness. The Lord is pleased in making known his own righteousness. For their sins, they were stripped of all their possessions. This fully came to pass in the destruction of the Jewish nation. There is no resisting nor escaping God’s anger. See the mischief sin makes; it provokes God to anger. And those not humbled by lesser judgments must expect greater. Alas! how many professed Christians are blind as the benighted heathen! While the Lord is pleased in saving sinners through the righteousness of Christ, he will also glorify his justice by punishing all proud despisers. Seeing God has poured out his wrath on his once-favored people because of their sins, let us fear lest a promise being left to us of entering into his rest, any of us should be found to come short of it.
